title: Emergent electric fields driven by phonon-coupled skyrmion resonances
abstract: We develop a coarse-grained theoretical description of the macroscopic emergent electric field generated by phonon-coupled lattice deformations in the breathing and rotational dynamics of a skyrmion lattice under microwave excitation. The analysis identifies the symmetry and dynamical conditions that yield rectified (dc) and oscillating (ac) electric fields, even in the absence of net translational motion of the skyrmion lattice, particularly in the dilute-lattice limit. Using experimentally measurable skyrmion profile parameters such as the equilibrium radius, domain-wall width, and dynamical resonance frequency of skyrmion lattice, the model further enables identification of harmonic components contributing to the observed macroscopic electrodynamic response in the long-wavelength phonon limit ($q \to 0$) and at finite phonon frequency, providing a unified framework for phonon-driven spin-charge-lattice coupling in topological magnets.
summary: This paper provides a unified theoretical framework for understanding how phonon-driven deformations in a skyrmion lattice generate macroscopic electric fields. It shows that breathing and rotational dynamics can produce measurable ac and dc electric signals even without moving the lattice. This is significant for developing new ways to control and detect topological magnetic textures via spin-charge-lattice coupling.
main_problem: The paper aims to describe the generation of macroscopic emergent electric fields resulting from the coupling between phonons and the breathing/rotational dynamics of a skyrmion lattice under microwave excitation.
main_result: The study identifies the specific symmetry and dynamical conditions required to produce both rectified (dc) and oscillating (ac) electric fields, even without net lattice translation.
method: The authors develop a coarse-grained theoretical framework to analyze the electrodynamic response in the long-wavelength phonon limit.
model_or_system: A skyrmion lattice subject to phonon-coupled lattice deformations and microwave excitation, specifically focusing on breathing and rotational modes.
key_observables: Macroscopic emergent electric fields (dc and ac components), skyrmion radius, domain-wall width, and dynamical resonance frequency.
important_parameters: Long-wavelength phonon limit (q -> 0), finite phonon frequency, and the dilute-lattice limit.
main_assumptions: The analysis utilizes a coarse-grained description and focuses on the long-wavelength limit of phonons.
paper_structure: The paper presents a theoretical development of a coarse-grained model, followed by an analysis of symmetry and dynamical conditions for electric field generation, and concludes with an identification of harmonic components in the electrodynamic response.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable

Spintronics.note: The paper directly addresses the interface of spin textures and electrodynamics by describing how phonon-driven skyrmion dynamics generate macroscopic electric signals.
